query: Matching Training-Free complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: Match Madness is impossible to do on the free version and is totally irrelevant to actual language learning. : r/duolingo
results.content:
5.   About the learning, as you, I also find it easier with the language I'm learning than with the ones I'm already fluent. But I guess that's mostly because we're not associating those specific words in the lessons like we're doing with the learning language. Even being fluent, the matching is harder than with words we've just seen in the path. So if you're scoring higher on Italian than on German, maybe your experiment proves quite the opposite - you're learning.

 are just not fast rather than your brain not being fast, and it’s ok, but the challenge is still good. Nobody is watching you so it’s ok if you don’t succeed. The goal is the process, forcing you to associate translations quickly, not the result of the game.

Image 34: u/Karaokebaren avatar

Karaokebaren

•2mo ago [...] Also side note: "you suck" ... really...? How old are you? I haven't heard or used that insult since I was like 16.

.

3.   It's a bit tough when you start, but you just get better and faster with time. Around 1 year here and I can regularly get on the level 12 (160 matches). Sometimes I even wish they had more levels, since I often end with 12-16 seconds left, that could fit more matches, for higher XP.

4.   I don't see a "coercion" into spending gems, since it doesn't block your path and learning. It's an optional tool for fun and some additional hard challenge, if you don't have enough skill for it right now or don't think is helpful, just move on and save your gems. Open menu Open navigation. The first star wasn't that difficult, but I failed to get enough matchups in time to earn the 2nd star. I tried over 10 times and failed.

So partly out of rage, partly out of scientific interest, I thought I'd test it using German instead (my native language besides English). I ended up failing too, scoring even lower.

results.title: ‎Woofz - Puppy and Dog Training App - App Store
results.content: did not match what the words said or were just confusing overall. Lastly, the plan is not continuous even though the subscription is continuous. My expectations were that if I am continuing to pay for content there will be new content being added to “my plan” after completing “my plan” I found out that that’s it. There are some side courses you can do with your dog but no continued courses like a level 1 level 2 format. Pretty disappointing I was really hoping to keep going and getting more advanced with the training. Essentially you get the same thing as a level 1 obedience class. [...] I had started using this app with the 7-day free trial. I continued to use the app after 7 days with the $40 subscription. The app itself was fine, easy to follow, as well as effective. It helped me train my puppy. No complaints with the app itself, that is the 2 stars. However, I had canceled my app (1-16)at least a week prior to my subscription due date, but was still charged the $40 fee on 1-24. Reaching out to their customer service has not been very helpful. They did respond back to me in a relatively timely manner, but basically called me a liar saying that my acct was effective until the day they received my email (1-25), but they had canceled it now & I could use it until end of month & wouldn’t be charged again. When I sent screen shots of my acct being canceled/expired by 1/16 [...] Hello Meggie! We're really sorry to hear that the app didn’t meet your expectations. We completely understand your frustration, especially when it comes to the personalization of the training plan. While we do offer tailored guidance, we recognize that some users, like yourself, may already have a dog with prior training, and we should have been more flexible with the customization options from the start.Your feedback is valuable, and we’ll make sure to take it into account as we continue to improve the app. We’re also sorry about the illustrations being confusing and the lack of advanced courses. We strive to provide a continuous learning experience, and we’ll work to expand the content for users who are looking for more advanced material.If you'd like assistance with adjusting your

results.title: HiFlow: Training-free High-Resolution Image Generation with Flow ...
results.content:
Refer to caption

Direction alignment. While initialization alignment enables low-frequent consistency at the beginning of high-resolution generation, such low-frequent information may be destroyed at subsequent denoising steps due to the limited stability of models in synthesizing high-resolution content (as shown in Fig. 2), resulting in broken structures. Therefore, the direction alignment is designed to preserve structure while adding details. This is achieved by modifying the denoising direction based on reference flow, which can be expressed as: [...] Flow Matching  and Rectified Flow  aim at streamlining the formulation of Ordinary Differential Equation (ODE) models by establishing a linear transition between two distinct distributions. Consider clean image samples X0∼π0similar-tosubscript𝑋0subscript𝜋0X\_{0}\sim\pi\_{0}italic\_X start\_POSTSUBSCRIPT 0 end\_POSTSUBSCRIPT ∼ italic\_π start\_POSTSUBSCRIPT 0 end\_POSTSUBSCRIPT and Gaussian noise X1∼π1similar-tosubscript𝑋1subscript𝜋1X\_{1}\sim\pi\_{1}italic\_X start\_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end\_POSTSUBSCRIPT ∼ italic\_π start\_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end\_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Rectified Flow delineates a linear trajectory from X1subscript𝑋1X\_{1}italic\_X start\_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end\_POSTSUBSCRIPT to X0subscript𝑋0X\_{0}italic\_X start\_POSTSUBSCRIPT 0 end\_POSTSUBSCRIPT, with the intermediate state [...] Baselines. The compared methods consist of various types of methods that enable high-resolution image generation, encompassing training-free approaches (DemoFusion , DiffuseHigh , I-Max ), training-based approaches (UltraPixel , Flux-Pro , DALL·E 3 ), and an image super-resolution method, BSRGAN . For a fair comparison, the training-free methods are tested on the same Flux model according to the official implementations.

results.title: Mix and Match: Learning-free Controllable Text Generation ...
results.content:
6 Conclusion We present Mix and Match Language Models (M&M LM), a training-free framework for con-trolled text generation that can easily mix heteroge-neous expert modules. We show that our framework outperforms prior methods on a suite of text revision and attribute-controlled generation tasks. Further, our results indicate that probabilistic energy language models, typically considered intractable, can be used for practical text generation tasks when combined with an appropriate sampling scheme.
Acknowledgments The authors would like to thank the anonymous reviewers and meta-reviewers for their helpful feedback.
We also thank our colleagues at the UCSD/CMU Berg Lab for their helpful comments and feedback. [...] either training domain-conditioned neu-ral language models (Prabhumoye et al., 2020; He et al., 2020; Lample et al., 2018; Shen et al., 2017; Krishna et al., 2020; Reif et al., 2021; Ficler and Goldberg, 2017; Khalifa et al., 2021) or finetun-ing/modifying an underlying large pre-trained base model for generation on domain-specific data for attribute sensitive generation (Ziegler et al., 2019; Keskar et al., 2019; Mai et al., 2020; Gururangan et al., 2020; Chronopoulou et al., 2021). Not only do these approaches involve computational overhead and estimation errors associated with the training of language models, but they are also dependent on ac-cess to a large amount of attribute-specific language data which can be impractical in many scenarios and exacerbate privacy concerns (Brown et [...] Our approach eschews training and focuses on generation-time control from pre-trained modules.
